name: Array Traversal
description: Visit each element in order using a loop.
exampleJava: for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) { sum += arr[i]; }
commonMistakes: Using the wrong loop boundary
Skipping the first or last element

name: Min and Max in Array
description: Track the best value seen so far while scanning the array once.
exampleJava: int best = arr[0]; for (int value : arr) best = Math.max(best, value);
commonMistakes: Initializing from 0 when array can contain negatives
Forgetting to handle a single-element array

name: Sorted Check
description: Compare each element with the next one to verify ordering.
exampleJava: for (int i = 1; i < arr.length; i++) if (arr[i] < arr[i - 1]) return false;
commonMistakes: Using > instead of < for ascending checks
Not handling arrays of length 0 or 1

name: Reverse Array
description: Swap elements from both ends until pointers meet.
exampleJava: while (left < right) { int t = arr[left]; arr[left++] = arr[right]; arr[right--] = t; }
commonMistakes: Not moving both pointers
Using extra memory when in-place reversal is expected

name: Second Largest
description: Track the largest and second largest values in one pass.
exampleJava: if (x > first) { second = first; first = x; } else if (x > second && x != first) { second = x; }
commonMistakes: Not handling duplicates carefully
Using sorting for a problem that expects one pass

name: Frequency Counting
description: Count how many times each value appears using a map or count array.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize missing counts
Using nested loops unnecessarily

name: Prefix Sum
description: Precompute running sums so range-sum queries become fast.
commonMistakes: Off-by-one errors in prefix indexing
Forgetting the extra 0 at the front

name: Kadane Algorithm
description: Track the best subarray ending at each index.
exampleJava: current = Math.max(arr[i], current + arr[i]); best = Math.max(best, current);
commonMistakes: Resetting to 0 incorrectly when all values are negative
Confusing current sum and best sum

name: Two Pointers
description: Maintain two moving indices to transform or scan an array efficiently.
commonMistakes: Moving only one pointer
Breaking sorted-array assumptions

name: In-place Array Update
description: Modify the same array without allocating another full answer array.
commonMistakes: Overwriting values too early
Returning the wrong final length

name: Sliding Window
description: Grow and shrink a window while tracking the answer incrementally.
exampleJava: sum += arr[right]; while (sum > k) sum -= arr[left++];
commonMistakes: Forgetting to shrink the window
Using this trick on arrays with negative numbers when it does not apply

name: Stock Profit Scan
description: Track the minimum buy price so far and the best profit so far.
exampleJava: minPrice = Math.min(minPrice, price); best = Math.max(best, price - minPrice);
commonMistakes: Buying after selling in the scan logic
Resetting the minimum incorrectly
